channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A year after the Baltimore bridge collapse, a long road to recovery is ahead
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A year after the catastrophic collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland leaders are honoring the six construction workers who were killed that night when the road they were repairing buckled underneath them.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KREX 5

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene fueled wildfires in North Carolina and South Carolina, where evacuation orders were in effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Video and 911 calls played by police detail chaos from deadly shooting in New Mexico
item.description: Police released video footage and 911 emergency calls Tuesday depicting chilling scenes of people running in panic as gunfire crackled in a New Mexico park where three people were killed and 15 others were wounded during an unauthorized car show.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia student protester can't be detained for now as she fights deportation, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Columbia University student from South Korea who is facing potential deportation for her involvement in a pro-Palestinian protest can't be taken into immigration detention for now, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Boston skating community hosts the world championships even as it mourns plane crash victims
item.description: NORWOOD, Mass. (AP) — The chairs where Jinna Han and Spencer Lane used to sit are still there, and covered with gifts: cards and cookies, plants and pictures — anything that might have sparked a connection for their friends and fellow skaters.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP-led states push for control of school aid as Trump promises a smaller federal role in education
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Governors in several Republican-led states are pressing the Trump administration to cut strings attached to their federal education money, a goal conservatives have long dreamed of that now appears within reach as President Donald Trump moves to dismantle the Education Department.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More states move to ban cellphones in school as Georgia is latest to act
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — More states are moving to ban or restrict cellphones in school, a trend that continues to win bipartisan support in legislatures.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump orders declassification of FBI’s Russia investigation
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed a memorandum directing the declassification of FBI files related to the investigation into his 2016 campaign's potential ties to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: New York sues Trump over Department of Education closure
item.description: “The Trump administration’s illegal cuts to the Department of Education are an attack on our educators, our schools, and our students,” New York AG Letitia James said.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione's attorney wants 'manifesto' to be inadmissible
item.description: Luigi Mangione's defense attorney has accused law enforcement of leaking the notebook to the media to stir panic.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Mega Millions is changing: What to know before its price change, game overhaul
item.description: More expensive tickets, faster-growing jackpots, and a built-in multiplier — here's what to expect when Mega Millions gets overhauled next month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Consider deleting your 23andMe data now, California AG urges
item.description: The genetic testing company has the genetic data of more than15 million customers — and California Attorney General Rob Bonta has advised users to purge their data.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jonas Brothers to record new theme song for Disneyland's 70th anniversary
item.description: The Jonas Brothers are bringing a bit of Disney magic to their setlist. On Sunday, the “Happiest Place on Earth” announced that the trio will record an upbeat new theme song to celebrate the resort’s upcoming 70th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Columbia student sues Trump admin over efforts to deport her
item.description: Columbia University student Yunseo Chung is suing the Trump administration amid its efforts to deport the 21-year-old, who has been in the U.S. since she was 7 and has lawful permanent resident status.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy resigns after 5 years in the position
item.description: The head of the beleaguered U.S. Postal Service, Louis DeJoy, resigned Monday after nearly five years in the position, leaving as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency have floated the idea of privatizing mail service.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dairy Queen celebrates 85 years with 85-cent Blizzard deal
item.description: Dairy Queen is celebrating its 85th anniversary with a sweet deal for its loyal fans. Until April 6, DQ Rewards members can enjoy a small Blizzard Treat for just 85 cents with a minimum purchase of $1—an exclusive offer in the DQ App.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to halt judge's order to rehire probationary federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Monday to halt a ruling ordering the rehiring of thousands of federal workers let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Deadly hit-and-run in New Mexico brings juvenile justice challenges into focus
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Behind the wheel of the stolen car was a 12-year-old boy. In the front passenger seat was a boy who just days earlier had marked his 11th birthday.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California AG warns 23andMe users to delete their data
item.description: The genetic testing company has the genetic data of more than15 million customers — and California Attorney General Rob Bonta has advised users to purge their data.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A new thermal steam vent is grabbing attention in ever-changing Yellowstone National Park
item.description: YELLOWSTONE NATIONAL PARK, Wyo. (AP) — A new thermal vent spewing steam in the air at Yellowstone National Park is gaining attention, mainly because it's visible from a road rather than any significant change in the park famous for its thousands of geysers, hot springs and bubbling mud pots.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dollywood parent company expands into Southern California, acquires Raging Waters, Castle Park
item.description: The parent company of Dollywood and Silver Dollar has expanded its footprint into Southern California for the first time after buying Raging Waters, Castle Park, and Boomers. Dollywood owner Herschend signed an agreement to buy Palace Entertainment’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he knows nothing of Atlantic journalist swept up in war plan text chain
item.description: President Trump said Monday he knew nothing of a report by a journalist for The Atlantic who says he was swept up in a text message chain with defense and national security officials while they detailed plans for an attack on Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Kidnapped 6-year-old girl discovered during traffic stop: troopers
item.description: A trooper's discovery during a routine traffic stop last month led to the rescue of a 6-year-old girl who had been kidnapped for weeks, according to the Kansas Highway Patrol.
item.dc:creator: Barry Owens, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Utah Rep. Mia Love, the first Black Republican woman elected to the US House, has died
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Former U.S. Rep. Mia Love of Utah, a daughter of Haitian immigrants who became the first Black Republican woman elected to Congress, died Sunday. She was 49. Love's family posted news of her death on Love's X account.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to intervene in rehiring of federal workers
item.description: The Trump administration Monday asked the Supreme Court to block an order forcing the government to rehire thousands of workers fired as part of President Trump’s effort to drastically reduce the size of the federal workforce. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Maps: The states, counties with the most excessive drinkers, per new report
item.description: "Excessive drinking" includes binge drinking, heavy drinking, drinking while pregnant, and underage drinking, according to the CDC.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods confirms relationship with Vanessa Trump
item.description: “Love is in the air and life is better with you by my side! We look forward to our journey through life together,” Woods said on X, where he has 6.4 million followers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Mass layoffs in the federal government raises concerns of potential espionage
item.description: As President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk work to overhaul the federal government, they’re forcing out thousands of workers with insider knowledge and connections who now need a job.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Son of former MLB outfielder Brett Gardner dies after falling ill on vacation
item.description: Former New York Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner's son, 14-year-old Miller Gardner, has died after falling ill while on vacation, the team announced Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 months into Trump's second administration, the news industry faces challenges from all directions
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — During the first Trump administration, the biggest concern for many journalists was labels. Would they, or their news outlet, be called “fake news” or an “enemy of the people” by a president and his supporters? They now face a more assertive President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fatal shooting in New Mexico park casts pall over Legislature and its ability to contain crime
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Efforts by New Mexico lawmakers to contain violent crime took center stage Saturday at the conclusion of an annual legislative session — just hours after three people were killed and 15 injured in an outburst of gunfire at a public park in Las Cruces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Viral videos of dogs called a 'Himalayan fur goblin' and 'teacup werewolf' boost adoptions
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — For over a decade, Adrian Budnick has taken adoption photos of the dogs at Nashville's county animal shelter, but it wasn't until the COVID pandemic that an idea came to her.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Yorkers scramble for free eggs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of people lined up Friday morning at three sites in New York City, some arriving more than an hour early, for the opportunity to snag one of the nation's hottest commodities: a dozen free eggs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
